description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.

subcriteria.name: ir_roles_process
subcriteria.reference_text: Incident response roles assigned including incident commander, security analyst, communications lead, and engineering responders. Runbook or playbook defines step-by-step containment and remediation procedures for common incident types.

subcriteria.name: containment_remediation
subcriteria.reference_text: Containment actions taken to isolate affected systems, revoke compromised credentials, and block malicious traffic. Remediation includes patching vulnerabilities, rotating secrets, and restoring from clean backups. Timeline of actions documented.

subcriteria.name: communication
subcriteria.reference_text: Communication protocols for notifying affected parties including customers, regulators, and internal leadership. Breach notification templates prepared. Incident status updates communicated via established channels.

subcriteria.name: post_incident_review
subcriteria.reference_text: Post-incident review conducted with blameless post-mortem. Root cause identified and corrective actions assigned. Incident response plan updated based on lessons learned. Follow-up testing validates remediation effectiveness.

mismatch_signals.reason: Change management is CC8.1, not incident response.
Vendor risk management is CC9.2, not incident response.
